sample_size.source_quote: 665 patients were eligible for suspicion of sepsis during the period between January 1st, 2019, to December 31, 2020.

patient_age_mean.source_quote: The average age in the sample was 73.36 years old and 75% of the patients were elderly (≥ 65 years).

antibiotic_timing_hours.source_quote: the mean time of ATB start was 37.43 min.

mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: sex, age, different qSOFA points, different severity criteria points, time to ATB, lactate, blood cultures, creatinine, platelets, administration of vasopressor
mortality_predictors.source_quote: Positive blood cultures (RR, 1.63;95% CI, 1.10 to 2.41) and administration of vasopressors in the ED (RR, 2.14;95% CI, 1.44 to 3.17) featured as independent factors of 30-day mortality in the multivariate analysis.

mortality_predictors.predictor_variable: Administration of vasopressors in the ED
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: sex, age, different qSOFA points, different severity criteria points, time to ATB, lactate, blood cultures, creatinine, platelets, administration of vasopressor
mortality_predictors.source_quote: Positive blood cultures (RR, 1.63;95% CI, 1.10 to 2.41) and administration of vasopressors in the ED (RR, 2.14;95% CI, 1.44 to 3.17) featured as independent factors of 30-day mortality in the multivariate analysis.

mortality_predictors.predictor_variable: Administration of vasopressors in the ED
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: sex, age, different qSOFA points, different severity criteria points, time to ATB, lactate, blood cultures, creatinine, platelets, administration of vasopressor
mortality_predictors.source_quote: Administration of vasopressors at the ED (RR, 2.25; CI 95%, 1.58 to 3.21) was the only variable that was considered an independent risk factor in the non-survival group in the multivariate analysis.

The highest AUROC was found for qSOFA (AUC = 0.58), and the lowest AUROC was found for the severity criteria (AUC = 0.52). A qSOFA score of two or more points was used as the reference baseline... The sensitivity for the qSOFA curve was 20% (95% CI 16%-24%), with a specificity of 87% (95% CI 82%-91%).

the lowest AUROC was found for the severity criteria (AUC = 0.52)... one or more points were used as the reference baseline for the severity criteria curve. The sensitivity for the severity criteria curve was 18% (95% CI 15%-22%), with a specificity of 85% (95% CI 80%-89%).

The univariate analysis has shown that age ≥ 65 years, three points on qSOFA, shorter times before ATB administration, and higher values of lactate were risk factors associated with higher mortality in 30 days.

The univariate analysis has shown that age ≥ 65 years, three points on qSOFA, shorter times before ATB administration, and higher values of lactate were risk factors associated with higher mortality in 30 days.

The univariate analysis has shown that age ≥ 65 years, three points on qSOFA, shorter times before ATB administration, and higher values of lactate were risk factors associated with higher mortality in 30 days.

shorter times before ATB administration... were risk factors associated with higher mortality in 30 days... When evaluating the three different antibiotic administration intervals (less than 30 min, 30–60 min, and more than 60 min) in relation to mortality, no statistical significance was found concerning survival rates (p = 0.21).
